   512→/// Coordinate descent solver for Lasso and Elastic Net regularization
   513→///
   514→/// This implements an efficient coordinate descent algorithm optimized for sparse solutions.
   515→/// Features parallel processing when beneficial and proper standardization handling.
   516→///
   517→/// # Arguments
   518→/// * `x` - Design matrix (n_observations × n_predictors)
   519→/// * `y` - Response vector (n_observations)
   520→/// * `config` - Coordinate descent configuration
   521→///
   522→/// # Returns
   523→/// * `OptimResult` containing final coefficients and convergence information
   524→pub fn coordinate_descent(
   525→    x: ArrayView2<f64>,
   526→    y: ArrayView1<f64>,
   527→    config: Option<CoordinateDescentConfig>,
   528→) -> Result<OptimResult, OptimError> {
   529→    let config = config.unwrap_or_default();
   530→    let (n_obs, n_vars) = x.dim();
   531→
   532→    if y.len() != n_obs {
   533→        return Err(OptimError::Numerical(
   534→            "Response vector length doesn't match design matrix rows".to_string(),
   535→        ));
   536→    }
   537→
   538→    // Standardize features if requested
   539→    let (x_std, x_means, x_scales) = if config.standardize {
   540→        standardize_features(&x)
   541→    } else {
   542→        (x.to_owned(), Array1::zeros(n_vars), Array1::ones(n_vars))
   543→    };
   544→
   545→    // Center response
   546→    let y_mean = y.mean().unwrap_or(0.0);
   547→    let y_centered = &y - y_mean;
   548→
   549→    // Initialize coefficients
   550→    let mut beta = Array1::zeros(n_vars);
   551→    let mut residuals = y_centered.clone();
   552→
   553→    // Pre-compute X'X diagonal for efficiency
   554→    let xtx_diag: Array1<f64> = x_std
   555→        .axis_iter(ndarray::Axis(1))
   556→        .map(|col| col.dot(&col))
   557→        .collect();
   558→
   559→    let mut prev_beta = beta.clone();
   560→
   561→    for iteration in 0..config.max_iter {
   562→        // Coordinate descent updates
   563→        for j in 0..n_vars {
   564→            if xtx_diag[j] == 0.0 {
   565→                continue; // Skip zero-variance features
   566→            }
   567→
   568→            // Calculate partial residual
   569→            let col_j = x_std.column(j);
   570→            let partial_residual = &residuals + beta[j] * &col_j;
   571→
   572→            // Calculate coordinate update
   573→            let xty = col_j.dot(&partial_residual);
   574→            let new_beta_j = soft_threshold(xty, config.lambda * config.alpha)
   575→                / (xtx_diag[j] + config.lambda * (1.0 - config.alpha));
   576→
   577→            // Update coefficient and residuals
   578→            let _delta = new_beta_j - beta[j];
   579→            beta[j] = new_beta_j;
   580→            residuals = &partial_residual - new_beta_j * &col_j;
   581→        }
   582→
   583→        // Check convergence
   584→        let max_change = (&beta - &prev_beta)
   585→            .mapv(f64::abs)
   586→            .fold(0.0f64, |acc, &val| acc.max(val));
   587→        if max_change < config.tolerance {
   588→            // Transform coefficients back to original scale
   589→            let final_beta = if config.standardize {
   590→                transform_coefficients_back(&beta, &x_means, &x_scales, y_mean)
   591→            } else {
   592→                beta
   593→            };
   594→
   595→            let linear_predictor = if config.standardize {
   596→                // final_beta includes intercept at position 0
   597→                let intercept = final_beta[0];
   598→                let slopes = Array1::from_iter(final_beta.iter().skip(1).cloned());
   599→                x.dot(&slopes) + intercept
   600→            } else {
   601→                x.dot(&final_beta)
   602→            };
   603→
   604→            return Ok(OptimResult {
   605→                params: final_beta,
   606→                iterations: iteration,
   607→                converged: true,
   608→                deviance: residuals.mapv(|r| r * r).sum(),
   609→                weights: Array1::ones(n_obs), // Unweighted for now
   610→                linear_predictor,
   611→            });
   612→        }
   613→
   614→        prev_beta.assign(&beta);
   615→    }
   616→
   617→    // Did not converge
   618→    let final_beta = if config.standardize {
   619→        transform_coefficients_back(&beta, &x_means, &x_scales, y_mean)
   620→    } else {
   621→        beta
   622→    };
   623→
   624→    let linear_predictor = if config.standardize {
   625→        // final_beta includes intercept at position 0
   626→        let intercept = final_beta[0];
   627→        let slopes = Array1::from_iter(final_beta.iter().skip(1).cloned());
   628→        x.dot(&slopes) + intercept
   629→    } else {
   630→        x.dot(&final_beta)
   631→    };
   632→
   633→    Ok(OptimResult {
   634→        params: final_beta,
   635→        iterations: config.max_iter,
   636→        converged: false,
   637→        deviance: residuals.mapv(|r| r * r).sum(),
   638→        weights: Array1::ones(n_obs),
   639→        linear_predictor,
   640→    })
   641→}
   642→
   643→/// Soft thresholding operator for L1 regularization
   644→fn soft_threshold(z: f64, gamma: f64) -> f64 {
   645→    if z > gamma {
   646→        z - gamma
   647→    } else if z < -gamma {
   648→        z + gamma
   649→    } else {
   650→        0.0
   651→    }
   652→}
   653→
   654→/// Standardize features to have mean 0 and variance 1
   655→fn standardize_features(x: &ArrayView2<f64>) -> (Array2<f64>, Array1<f64>, Array1<f64>) {
   656→    let (_n_obs, n_vars) = x.dim();
   657→    let mut x_std = x.to_owned();
   658→    let mut means = Array1::zeros(n_vars);
   659→    let mut scales = Array1::ones(n_vars);
   660→
   661→    for j in 0..n_vars {
   662→        let col = x.column(j);
   663→        let mean = col.mean().unwrap_or(0.0);
   664→        let var = col.mapv(|x| (x - mean).powi(2)).mean().unwrap_or(1.0);
   665→        let scale = var.sqrt();
   666→
   667→        means[j] = mean;
   668→        scales[j] = if scale > 1e-12 { scale } else { 1.0 };
   669→
   670→        // Standardize column
   671→        for i in 0..col.len() {
   672→            x_std[[i, j]] = (x_std[[i, j]] - mean) / scales[j];
   673→        }
   674→    }
   675→
   676→    (x_std, means, scales)
   677→}
   678→
   679→/// Transform coefficients back to original scale after standardization
   680→fn transform_coefficients_back(
   681→    beta_std: &Array1<f64>,
   682→    x_means: &Array1<f64>,
   683→    x_scales: &Array1<f64>,
   684→    y_mean: f64,
   685→) -> Array1<f64> {
   686→    let n_vars = beta_std.len();
   687→    let mut beta = Array1::zeros(n_vars + 1); // Include intercept
   688→
   689→    // Transform slope coefficients
   690→    for j in 0..n_vars {
   691→        beta[j + 1] = beta_std[j] / x_scales[j];
   692→    }
   693→
   694→    // Calculate intercept
   695→    let intercept_adjustment: f64 = (0..n_vars).map(|j| beta[j + 1] * x_means[j]).sum();
   696→    beta[0] = y_mean - intercept_adjustment;
   697→
   698→    beta
   699→}
   700→
   701→/// Anderson acceleration for faster IRLS convergence
   702→///
   703→/// This advanced acceleration method can significantly reduce iterations
   704→/// needed for GLM convergence, especially for difficult cases.
   705→fn apply_anderson_acceleration(
   706→    current_beta: &Array1<f64>,
   707→    new_beta: &Array1<f64>,
   708→    beta_history: &mut Vec<Array1<f64>>,
   709→    _iteration: usize,
   710→) -> Result<Array1<f64>, OptimError> {
   711→    // Store current iteration
   712→    beta_history.push(current_beta.clone());
   713→
   714→    // Keep only last 3 iterations for memory efficiency
   715→    if beta_history.len() > 3 {
   716→        beta_history.remove(0);
   717→    }
   718→
   719→    if beta_history.len() < 2 {
   720→        return Ok(new_beta.clone());
   721→    }
   722→
   723→    // Simplified Anderson acceleration (AA-1)
   724→    // In full implementation, would use QR decomposition for stability
   725→    let g_curr = new_beta - current_beta;
   726→    let g_prev = &beta_history[beta_history.len() - 1] - &beta_history[beta_history.len() - 2];
   727→
   728→    let diff = &g_curr - &g_prev;
   729→    let norm_sq = diff.mapv(|x| x * x).sum();
   730→
   731→    if norm_sq < 1e-12 {
   732→        return Ok(new_beta.clone());
   733→    }
   734→
   735→    // Anderson parameter
   736→    let alpha = g_curr.dot(&diff) / norm_sq;
   737→    let alpha = alpha.clamp(0.0, 1.0); // Safeguard
   738→
   739→    // Accelerated update
   740→    let accelerated = (1.0 - alpha) * new_beta + alpha * &beta_history[beta_history.len() - 1];
   741→
   742→    Ok(accelerated)
   743→}
   744→
   745→/// Adaptive step size adjustment for improved convergence
   746→fn adaptive_step_size(current_step: f64, prev_deviance: f64, current_deviance: f64) -> f64 {
   747→    if current_deviance < prev_deviance {
   748→        // Good step, can potentially increase
   749→        (current_step * 1.1).min(1.0)
   750→    } else {
   751→        // Bad step, decrease
   752→        (current_step * 0.7).max(0.1)
   753→    }
   754→}

   756→/// Regularization path results containing solutions for multiple lambda values
   757→#[derive(Debug)]
   758→pub struct RegularizationPathResult {
   759→    /// Lambda values used in the path
   760→    pub lambda_path: Array1<f64>,
   761→    /// Coefficients for each lambda (n_lambda × n_features)
   762→    pub coefficients_path: Array2<f64>,
   763→    /// Deviances for each lambda
   764→    pub deviances: Array1<f64>,
   765→    /// Number of non-zero coefficients for each lambda
   766→    pub n_nonzero: Array1<usize>,
   767→    /// Number of iterations for each lambda
   768→    pub iterations: Array1<usize>,
   769→    /// Fraction of deviance explained for each lambda
   770→    pub dev_ratio: Array1<f64>,
   771→}
   772→
   773→/// Compute efficient regularization path using coordinate descent
   774→///
   775→/// This implementation surpasses glmnet in speed through:
   776→/// - Intelligent lambda sequence computation
   777→/// - Warm starts between lambda values  
   778→/// - Early termination based on deviance saturation
   779→/// - Memory-efficient coefficient tracking
   780→/// - Strong rules for feature screening
   781→pub fn regularization_path(
   782→    x: ArrayView2<f64>,
   783→    y: ArrayView1<f64>,
   784→    config: Option<RegularizationPathConfig>,
   785→) -> Result<RegularizationPathResult, OptimError> {
   786→    let config = config.unwrap_or_default();
   787→    let (n_obs, n_vars) = x.dim();
   788→
   789→    if y.len() != n_obs {
   790→        return Err(OptimError::Numerical(
   791→            "Response vector length doesn't match design matrix rows".to_string(),
   792→        ));
   793→    }
   794→
   795→    // Standardize features if requested
   796→    let (x_std, x_means, x_scales) = if config.standardize {
   797→        standardize_features(&x)
   798→    } else {
   799→        (x.to_owned(), Array1::zeros(n_vars), Array1::ones(n_vars))
   800→    };
   801→
   802→    // Center response
   803→    let y_mean = y.mean().unwrap_or(0.0);
   804→    let y_centered = &y - y_mean;
   805→
   806→    // Compute lambda_max (smallest lambda that gives all-zero solution)
   807→    let lambda_max = compute_lambda_max(&x_std, &y_centered, config.alpha);
   808→    let lambda_min = lambda_max * config.lambda_min_ratio;
   809→
   810→    // Generate lambda sequence (logarithmic spacing)
   811→    let lambda_path = generate_lambda_sequence(lambda_max, lambda_min, config.n_lambda);
   812→
   813→    // Initialize result storage
   814→    let mut coefficients_path = Array2::zeros((config.n_lambda, n_vars + 1)); // +1 for intercept
   815→    let mut deviances = Array1::zeros(config.n_lambda);
   816→    let mut n_nonzero = Array1::zeros(config.n_lambda);
   817→    let mut iterations = Array1::zeros(config.n_lambda);
   818→    let mut dev_ratio = Array1::zeros(config.n_lambda);
   819→
   820→    // Calculate null deviance for dev_ratio computation
   821→    let null_deviance = y_centered.mapv(|x| x * x).sum();
   822→
   823→    // Initialize coefficients for warm starts
   824→    let mut beta_warm = Array1::zeros(n_vars);
   825→    let mut prev_deviance = null_deviance;
   826→
   827→    // Iterate through lambda path
   828→    for (lambda_idx, &current_lambda) in lambda_path.iter().enumerate() {
   829→        // Configure coordinate descent for this lambda
   830→        let cd_config = CoordinateDescentConfig {
   831→            max_iter: config.max_iter,
   832→            tolerance: config.tolerance,
   833→            lambda: current_lambda,
   834→            alpha: config.alpha,
   835→            standardize: false, // Already standardized
   836→        };
   837→
   838→        // Use warm start if enabled and not first iteration
   839→        let start_beta = if config.warm_start && lambda_idx > 0 {
   840→            Some(beta_warm.clone())
   841→        } else {
   842→            None
   843→        };
   844→
   845→        // Solve for current lambda
   846→        let result =
   847→            coordinate_descent_with_start(&x_std, &y_centered, start_beta, Some(cd_config))?;
   848→
   849→        // Transform coefficients back to original scale
   850→        let full_beta = transform_coefficients_back(&result.params, &x_means, &x_scales, y_mean);
   851→
   852→        // Store results
   853→        coefficients_path.row_mut(lambda_idx).assign(&full_beta);
   854→        deviances[lambda_idx] = result.deviance;
   855→        n_nonzero[lambda_idx] = result.params.iter().filter(|&&x| x.abs() > 1e-8).count();
   856→        iterations[lambda_idx] = result.iterations;
   857→        dev_ratio[lambda_idx] = 1.0 - result.deviance / null_deviance;
   858→
   859→        // Update warm start coefficients
   860→        beta_warm = result.params.clone();
   861→
   862→        // Early termination based on deviance saturation
   863→        if lambda_idx > 0 {
   864→            let frac_change = (prev_deviance - result.deviance) / prev_deviance;
   865→            if frac_change < config.min_frac_dev_change {
   866→                // Truncate arrays at current position
   867→                let actual_length = lambda_idx + 1;
   868→                let mut truncated_lambda = Array1::zeros(actual_length);
   869→                let mut truncated_coef = Array2::zeros((actual_length, n_vars + 1));
   870→                let mut truncated_dev = Array1::zeros(actual_length);
   871→                let mut truncated_nonzero = Array1::zeros(actual_length);
   872→                let mut truncated_iter = Array1::zeros(actual_length);
   873→                let mut truncated_ratio = Array1::zeros(actual_length);
   874→
   875→                for i in 0..actual_length {
   876→                    truncated_lambda[i] = lambda_path[i];
   877→                    truncated_coef.row_mut(i).assign(&coefficients_path.row(i));
   878→                    truncated_dev[i] = deviances[i];
   879→                    truncated_nonzero[i] = n_nonzero[i];
   880→                    truncated_iter[i] = iterations[i];
   881→                    truncated_ratio[i] = dev_ratio[i];
   882→                }
   883→
   884→                return Ok(RegularizationPathResult {
   885→                    lambda_path: truncated_lambda,
   886→                    coefficients_path: truncated_coef,
   887→                    deviances: truncated_dev,
   888→                    n_nonzero: truncated_nonzero,
   889→                    iterations: truncated_iter,
   890→                    dev_ratio: truncated_ratio,
   891→                });
   892→            }
   893→        }
   894→        prev_deviance = result.deviance;
   895→    }
   896→
   897→    Ok(RegularizationPathResult {
   898→        lambda_path,
   899→        coefficients_path,
   900→        deviances,
   901→        n_nonzero,
   902→        iterations,
   903→        dev_ratio,
   904→    })
   905→}
   906→
   907→/// Compute lambda_max (threshold for all-zero solution)
   908→fn compute_lambda_max(x: &Array2<f64>, y: &Array1<f64>, alpha: f64) -> f64 {
   909→    let max_correlation = x
   910→        .axis_iter(ndarray::Axis(1))
   911→        .map(|col| col.dot(y).abs())
   912→        .fold(0.0, f64::max);
   913→
   914→    max_correlation / alpha
   915→}
   916→
   917→/// Generate logarithmically spaced lambda sequence
   918→fn generate_lambda_sequence(lambda_max: f64, lambda_min: f64, n_lambda: usize) -> Array1<f64> {
   919→    let log_max = lambda_max.ln();
   920→    let log_min = lambda_min.ln();
   921→    let step = (log_max - log_min) / (n_lambda - 1) as f64;
   922→
   923→    Array1::from_iter((0..n_lambda).map(|i| (log_max - i as f64 * step).exp()))
   924→}
   925→
   926→/// Coordinate descent with optional starting values for warm starts
   927→fn coordinate_descent_with_start(
   928→    x: &Array2<f64>,
   929→    y: &Array1<f64>,
   930→    start_beta: Option<Array1<f64>>,
   931→    config: Option<CoordinateDescentConfig>,
   932→) -> Result<OptimResult, OptimError> {
   933→    let config = config.unwrap_or_default();
   934→    let (n_obs, n_vars) = x.dim();
   935→
   936→    // Initialize coefficients
   937→    let mut beta = start_beta.unwrap_or_else(|| Array1::zeros(n_vars));
   938→    if beta.len() != n_vars {
   939→        beta = Array1::zeros(n_vars); // Fallback for dimension mismatch
   940→    }
   941→
   942→    let mut residuals = y - &x.dot(&beta);
   943→
   944→    // Pre-compute X'X diagonal for efficiency
   945→    let xtx_diag: Array1<f64> = x
   946→        .axis_iter(ndarray::Axis(1))
   947→        .map(|col| col.dot(&col))
   948→        .collect();
   949→
   950→    let mut prev_beta = beta.clone();
   951→
   952→    for iteration in 0..config.max_iter {
   953→        // Coordinate descent updates
   954→        for j in 0..n_vars {
   955→            if xtx_diag[j] == 0.0 {
   956→                continue; // Skip zero-variance features
   957→            }
   958→
   959→            // Calculate partial residual
   960→            let col_j = x.column(j);
   961→            let partial_residual = &residuals + beta[j] * &col_j;
   962→
   963→            // Calculate coordinate update
   964→            let xty = col_j.dot(&partial_residual);
   965→            let new_beta_j = soft_threshold(xty, config.lambda * config.alpha)
   966→                / (xtx_diag[j] + config.lambda * (1.0 - config.alpha));
   967→
   968→            // Update coefficient and residuals
   969→            let delta = new_beta_j - beta[j];
   970→            beta[j] = new_beta_j;
   971→            residuals = &residuals - delta * &col_j;
   972→        }
   973→
   974→        // Check convergence
   975→        let param_change = (&beta - &prev_beta).mapv(|x| x.abs()).sum() / n_vars as f64;
   976→        if param_change < config.tolerance {
   977→            let deviance = residuals.mapv(|x| x * x).sum();
   978→            let linear_pred = x.dot(&beta);
   979→            return Ok(OptimResult {
   980→                params: beta,
   981→                iterations: iteration,
   982→                converged: true,
   983→                deviance,
   984→                weights: Array1::ones(n_obs), // Not meaningful for coordinate descent
   985→                linear_predictor: linear_pred,
   986→            });
   987→        }
   988→
   989→        prev_beta = beta.clone();
   990→    }
   991→
   992→    // Did not converge
   993→    let deviance = residuals.mapv(|x| x * x).sum();
   994→    let linear_pred = x.dot(&beta);
   995→    Ok(OptimResult {
   996→        params: beta,
   997→        iterations: config.max_iter,
   998→        converged: false,
   999→        deviance,
  1000→        weights: Array1::ones(n_obs),
  1001→        linear_predictor: linear_pred,
  1002→    })
  1003→}
  1004→
  1005→/// Configuration for L-BFGS-B optimization
  1006→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
  1007→pub struct LbfgsbConfig {
  1008→    /// Maximum iterations
  1009→    pub max_iter: usize,
  1010→    /// Convergence tolerance for gradient norm
  1011→    pub tolerance: f64,
  1012→    /// Number of corrections for L-BFGS (typically 3-20)
  1013→    pub m: usize,
  1014→    /// Lower bounds for parameters (None = -infinity)
  1015→    pub lower_bounds: Option<Array1<f64>>,
  1016→    /// Upper bounds for parameters (None = +infinity)
  1017→    pub upper_bounds: Option<Array1<f64>>,
  1018→    /// Armijo parameter for line search (typical: 1e-4)
  1019→    pub armijo: f64,
  1020→    /// Wolfe parameter for line search (typical: 0.9)
  1021→    pub wolfe: f64,
  1022→}
  1023→
  1024→impl Default for LbfgsbConfig {
  1025→    fn default() -> Self {
  1026→        Self {
  1027→            max_iter: 100,
  1028→            tolerance: 1e-8,
  1029→            m: 10,
  1030→            lower_bounds: None,
  1031→            upper_bounds: None,
  1032→            armijo: 1e-4,
  1033→            wolfe: 0.9,
  1034→        }
  1035→    }
  1036→}
  1037→
  1038→/// L-BFGS-B: Limited memory BFGS with box constraints
  1039→///
  1040→/// This implements the L-BFGS-B algorithm for minimizing a smooth function
  1041→/// subject to box constraints on the variables.
  1042→///
  1043→/// # Arguments
  1044→/// * `objective_fn` - Function that computes the objective value
  1045→/// * `gradient_fn` - Function that computes the gradient
  1046→/// * `x0` - Initial parameter values
  1047→/// * `config` - Optional configuration
  1048→///
  1049→/// # Returns
  1050→/// Optimization result with final parameters and convergence info
  1051→pub fn lbfgsb<F, G>(
  1052→    objective_fn: F,
  1053→    gradient_fn: G,
  1054→    x0: Array1<f64>,
  1055→    config: Option<LbfgsbConfig>,
  1056→) -> Result<OptimResult, OptimError>
  1057→where
  1058→    F: Fn(&Array1<f64>) -> f64,
  1059→    G: Fn(&Array1<f64>) -> Array1<f64>,
  1060→{
  1061→    let config = config.unwrap_or_default();
  1062→    let n = x0.len();
  1063→    
  1064→    // Initialize parameters
  1065→    let mut x = x0.clone();
  1066→    let mut f = objective_fn(&x);
  1067→    let mut g = gradient_fn(&x);
  1068→    
  1069→    // Project initial point onto feasible region
  1070→    project_onto_box(&mut x, &config.lower_bounds, &config.upper_bounds);
  1071→    
  1072→    // L-BFGS memory
  1073→    let mut s_list: Vec<Array1<f64>> = Vec::with_capacity(config.m);
  1074→    let mut y_list: Vec<Array1<f64>> = Vec::with_capacity(config.m);
  1075→    let mut rho_list: Vec<f64> = Vec::with_capacity(config.m);
  1076→    
  1077→    // Main optimization loop
  1078→    for iter in 0..config.max_iter {
  1079→        // Check convergence
  1080→        let g_proj = projected_gradient(&x, &g, &config.lower_bounds, &config.upper_bounds);
  1081→        let grad_norm = g_proj.mapv(|x| x * x).sum().sqrt();
  1082→        
  1083→        if grad_norm < config.tolerance {
  1084→            return Ok(OptimResult {
  1085→                params: x,
  1086→                iterations: iter,
  1087→                converged: true,
  1088→                deviance: f,
  1089→                weights: Array1::ones(n),
  1090→                linear_predictor: Array1::zeros(n),
  1091→            });
  1092→        }
  1093→        
  1094→        // Compute search direction using L-BFGS
  1095→        let d = lbfgs_direction(&g, &s_list, &y_list, &rho_list);
  1096→        
  1097→        // Line search with projection
  1098→        let (alpha, x_new, f_new, g_new) = projected_line_search(
  1099→            &objective_fn,
  1100→            &gradient_fn,
  1101→            &x,
  1102→            &d,
  1103→            f,
  1104→            &g,
  1105→            &config.lower_bounds,
  1106→            &config.upper_bounds,
  1107→            config.armijo,
  1108→            config.wolfe,
  1109→        )?;
  1110→        
  1111→        // Update L-BFGS memory
  1112→        if alpha > 0.0 {
  1113→            let s = &x_new - &x;
  1114→            let y = &g_new - &g;
  1115→            let rho = 1.0 / y.dot(&s).max(1e-20);
  1116→            
  1117→            // Add to memory (FIFO if at capacity)
  1118→            if s_list.len() >= config.m {
  1119→                s_list.remove(0);
  1120→                y_list.remove(0);
  1121→                rho_list.remove(0);
  1122→            }
  1123→            s_list.push(s);
  1124→            y_list.push(y);
  1125→            rho_list.push(rho);
  1126→        }
  1127→        
  1128→        // Update state
  1129→        x = x_new;
  1130→        f = f_new;
  1131→        g = g_new;
  1132→    }
  1133→    
  1134→    // Did not converge
  1135→    Ok(OptimResult {
  1136→        params: x,
  1137→        iterations: config.max_iter,
  1138→        converged: false,
  1139→        deviance: f,
  1140→        weights: Array1::ones(n),
  1141→        linear_predictor: Array1::zeros(n),
  1142→    })
  1143→}
  1144→
  1145→/// Project point onto box constraints
  1146→fn project_onto_box(
  1147→    x: &mut Array1<f64>,
  1148→    lower: &Option<Array1<f64>>,
  1149→    upper: &Option<Array1<f64>>,
  1150→) {
  1151→    if let Some(lb) = lower {
  1152→        for i in 0..x.len() {
  1153→            if x[i] < lb[i] {
  1154→                x[i] = lb[i];
  1155→            }
  1156→        }
  1157→    }
  1158→    
  1159→    if let Some(ub) = upper {
  1160→        for i in 0..x.len() {
  1161→            if x[i] > ub[i] {
  1162→                x[i] = ub[i];
  1163→            }
  1164→        }
  1165→    }
  1166→}
  1167→
  1168→/// Compute projected gradient for convergence check
  1169→fn projected_gradient(
  1170→    x: &Array1<f64>,
  1171→    g: &Array1<f64>,
  1172→    lower: &Option<Array1<f64>>,
  1173→    upper: &Option<Array1<f64>>,
  1174→) -> Array1<f64> {
  1175→    let mut g_proj = g.clone();
  1176→    
  1177→    for i in 0..x.len() {
  1178→        // Check if at lower bound
  1179→        if let Some(lb) = lower {
  1180→            if x[i] <= lb[i] && g[i] > 0.0 {
  1181→                g_proj[i] = 0.0;
  1182→            }
  1183→        }
  1184→        
  1185→        // Check if at upper bound
  1186→        if let Some(ub) = upper {
  1187→            if x[i] >= ub[i] && g[i] < 0.0 {
  1188→                g_proj[i] = 0.0;
  1189→            }
  1190→        }
  1191→    }
  1192→    
  1193→    g_proj
  1194→}
  1195→
  1196→/// Compute L-BFGS search direction
  1197→fn lbfgs_direction(
  1198→    g: &Array1<f64>,
  1199→    s_list: &[Array1<f64>],
  1200→    y_list: &[Array1<f64>],
  1201→    rho_list: &[f64],
  1202→) -> Array1<f64> {
  1203→    let mut d = -g.clone();
  1204→    let m = s_list.len();
  1205→    
  1206→    if m == 0 {
  1207→        return d;
  1208→    }
  1209→    
  1210→    // Two-loop recursion
  1211→    let mut alpha = vec![0.0; m];
  1212→    
  1213→    // First loop
  1214→    for i in (0..m).rev() {
  1215→        alpha[i] = rho_list[i] * s_list[i].dot(&d);
  1216→        d = d - alpha[i] * &y_list[i];
  1217→    }
  1218→    
  1219→    // Scale by initial Hessian approximation
  1220→    let gamma = s_list[m - 1].dot(&y_list[m - 1]) / y_list[m - 1].dot(&y_list[m - 1]);
  1221→    d = gamma * d;
  1222→    
  1223→    // Second loop
  1224→    for i in 0..m {
  1225→        let beta = rho_list[i] * y_list[i].dot(&d);
  1226→        d = d + (alpha[i] - beta) * &s_list[i];
  1227→    }
  1228→    
  1229→    d
  1230→}
  1231→
  1232→/// Line search with projection for box constraints
  1233→fn projected_line_search<F, G>(
  1234→    objective_fn: &F,
  1235→    gradient_fn: &G,
  1236→    x: &Array1<f64>,
  1237→    d: &Array1<f64>,
  1238→    f: f64,
  1239→    g: &Array1<f64>,
  1240→    lower: &Option<Array1<f64>>,
  1241→    upper: &Option<Array1<f64>>,
  1242→    armijo: f64,
  1243→    wolfe: f64,
  1244→) -> Result<(f64, Array1<f64>, f64, Array1<f64>), OptimError>
  1245→where
  1246→    F: Fn(&Array1<f64>) -> f64,
  1247→    G: Fn(&Array1<f64>) -> Array1<f64>,
  1248→{
  1249→    let max_steps = 20;
  1250→    let mut alpha = 1.0;
  1251→    
  1252→    for _ in 0..max_steps {
  1253→        // Compute new point with projection
  1254→        let mut x_new = x + alpha * d;
  1255→        project_onto_box(&mut x_new, lower, upper);
  1256→        
  1257→        // Evaluate at new point
  1258→        let f_new = objective_fn(&x_new);
  1259→        let g_new = gradient_fn(&x_new);
  1260→        
  1261→        // Check Armijo condition
  1262→        let expected_decrease = armijo * g.dot(&(&x_new - x));
  1263→        if f_new <= f + expected_decrease {
  1264→            // Check Wolfe condition
  1265→            let curvature = g_new.dot(d);
  1266→            if curvature >= wolfe * g.dot(d) {
  1267→                return Ok((alpha, x_new, f_new, g_new));
  1268→            }
  1269→        }
  1270→        
  1271→        // Backtrack
  1272→        alpha *= 0.5;
  1273→        
  1274→        if alpha < 1e-10 {
  1275→            // Return current point if line search fails
  1276→            return Ok((0.0, x.clone(), f, g.clone()));
  1277→        }
  1278→    }
  1279→    
  1280→    Err(OptimError::Numerical("Line search failed".to_string()))
  1281→}
  1282→
  1283→#[cfg(test)]
  1284→mod tests {
  1285→    use super::*;
  1286→    use ndarray::array;
  1287→
  1288→    #[test]
  1289→    fn test_lbfgsb_unconstrained() {
  1290→        // Test minimizing a simple quadratic function: f(x) = x^T * x
  1291→        let objective = |x: &Array1<f64>| -> f64 { x.dot(x) };
  1292→        let gradient = |x: &Array1<f64>| -> Array1<f64> { 2.0 * x };
  1293→
  1294→        let x0 = array![1.0, 2.0, 3.0];
  1295→        let result = lbfgsb(objective, gradient, x0, None).unwrap();
  1296→
  1297→        assert!(result.converged);
  1298→        assert!(result.params.mapv(f64::abs).sum() < 1e-6);
  1299→        assert!(result.deviance < 1e-12);
  1300→    }
  1301→
  1302→    #[test]
  1303→    fn test_lbfgsb_box_constrained() {
  1304→        // Test minimizing f(x) = (x-2)^2 with constraint x >= 3
  1305→        let objective = |x: &Array1<f64>| -> f64 {
  1306→            let diff = x[0] - 2.0;
  1307→            diff * diff
  1308→        };
  1309→        let gradient = |x: &Array1<f64>| -> Array1<f64> { array![2.0 * (x[0] - 2.0)] };
  1310→
  1311→        let x0 = array![0.0];
  1312→        let config = LbfgsbConfig {
  1313→            lower_bounds: Some(array![3.0]),
  1314→            upper_bounds: None,
  1315→            ..Default::default()
  1316→        };
  1317→
  1318→        let result = lbfgsb(objective, gradient, x0, Some(config)).unwrap();
  1319→
  1320→        assert!(result.converged);
  1321→        assert!((result.params[0] - 3.0).abs() < 1e-6);
  1322→    }
  1323→
  1324→    #[test]
  1325→    fn test_lbfgsb_bounded_quadratic() {
  1326→        // Test minimizing f(x,y) = x^2 + y^2 with bounds -1 <= x,y <= 2
  1327→        let objective = |x: &Array1<f64>| -> f64 { x[0] * x[0] + x[1] * x[1] };
  1328→        let gradient = |x: &Array1<f64>| -> Array1<f64> { array![2.0 * x[0], 2.0 * x[1]] };
  1329→
  1330→        let x0 = array![3.0, -2.0]; // Start outside bounds
  1331→        let config = LbfgsbConfig {
  1332→            lower_bounds: Some(array![-1.0, -1.0]),
  1333→            upper_bounds: Some(array![2.0, 2.0]),

  1341→        assert!(result.params.mapv(f64::abs).sum() < 1e-6);

content.text: Now let me examine the stats_models module to understand how the coordinate descent is used:

content.content:
1→//! High-level statistical modeling API for Rust
     2→//!
     3→//! This crate provides familiar model-fitting APIs like `Lm` (Linear Models)
     4→//! and `Glm` (Generalized Linear Models), integrating lower-level crates
     5→//! to provide a complete modeling workflow.
     6→
     7→#![forbid(unsafe_code)]
     8→#![warn(missing_docs)]
     9→
    10→use faer::{prelude::*, Mat};
    11→use family::Family;
    12→use link::LinkFn;
    13→use ndarray::{Array1, ArrayView1, ArrayView2};
    14→use optimcore::{coordinate_descent, irls, CoordinateDescentConfig, IrlsConfig};
    15→use resample::{bootstrap, BootstrapConfig, BootstrapResult, BootstrapStatistic};
    16→
    17→/// Statistical modeling errors
    18→#[derive(thiserror::Error, Debug)]
    19→pub enum ModelError {
    20→    /// Linear algebra computation error
    21→    #[error("Linear algebra error: {0}")]
    22→    LinearAlgebra(String),
    23→    /// Invalid model specification
    24→    #[error("Invalid model: {0}")]
    25→    InvalidModel(String),
    26→}
    27→
    28→// Re-export important types for convenience
    29→pub use family::{Binomial, Gaussian, Poisson};
    30→pub use link::{Identity, Log, Logit};
    31→
    32→/// Common trait for all fitted models
    33→pub trait Fit {
    34→    /// Get model coefficients
    35→    fn coefficients(&self) -> &[f64];
    36→
    37→    /// Get fitted values
    38→    fn fitted(&self) -> &[f64];
    39→
    40→    /// Get residuals
    41→    fn residuals(&self) -> &[f64];
    42→
    43→    /// Get model summary
    44→    fn summary(&self) -> ModelSummary;
    45→
    46→    /// Perform bootstrap resampling on the model
    47→    fn bootstrap(&self, _n_samples: usize) -> Result<BootstrapResult, ModelError> {
    48→        Err(ModelError::InvalidModel(
    49→            "Bootstrap not implemented for this model type".to_string(),
    50→        ))
    51→    }
    52→}
    53→
    54→/// Model summary information
    55→#[derive(Debug)]
    56→pub struct ModelSummary {
    57→    /// Coefficient table
    58→    pub coefficients: Vec<CoefficientRow>,
    59→    /// R-squared (coefficient of determination)
    60→    pub r_squared: f64,
    61→    /// Adjusted R-squared
    62→    pub adj_r_squared: f64,
    63→    /// Residual standard error
    64→    pub residual_std_error: f64,
    65→    /// F-statistic for overall model significance
    66→    pub f_statistic: f64,
    67→    /// p-value for F-statistic
    68→    pub f_p_value: f64,
    69→    /// Degrees of freedom for model (number of predictors excluding intercept)
    70→    pub df_model: usize,
    71→    /// Degrees of freedom for residuals
    72→    pub df_residual: usize,
    73→}
    74→
    75→/// A row in the coefficient table
    76→#[derive(Debug)]
    77→pub struct CoefficientRow {
    78→    /// Variable name
    79→    pub name: String,
    80→    /// Estimated coefficient
    81→    pub estimate: f64,
    82→    /// Standard error
    83→    pub std_error: f64,
    84→    /// t-statistic
    85→    pub t_value: f64,
    86→    /// p-value
    87→    pub p_value: f64,
    88→}
    89→
    90→/// Linear model fitting
    91→pub struct Lm;
    92→
    93→/// Generalized linear model fitting
    94→pub struct Glm;
    95→
    96→/// Lasso regression (L1 regularization)
    97→pub struct Lasso;
    98→
    99→/// Ridge regression (L2 regularization)
   100→pub struct Ridge;
   101→
   102→/// Fitted linear model results
   103→#[derive(Debug)]
   104→pub struct LmFit {
   105→    coefficients: Vec<f64>,
   106→    fitted_values: Vec<f64>,
   107→    residuals: Vec<f64>,
   108→    /// Number of observations
   109→    n_obs: usize,
   110→    /// Matrix rank (for rank-deficient matrices)
   111→    rank: usize,
   112→    /// Design matrix for statistical inference (stored for covariance calculation)
   113→    x_matrix: Mat<f64>,
   114→}
   115→
   116→/// Fitted generalized linear model results
   117→#[derive(Debug)]
   118→pub struct GlmFit {
   119→    coefficients: Vec<f64>,
   120→    fitted_values: Vec<f64>,
   121→    residuals: Vec<f64>,
   122→    /// Linear predictor values (X * β)
   123→    linear_predictor: Vec<f64>,
   124→    /// Number of observations
   125→    n_obs: usize,
   126→    /// Design matrix for statistical inference
   127→    x_matrix: Mat<f64>,
   128→    /// Final deviance
   129→    deviance: f64,
   130→    /// Working weights from final IRLS iteration
   131→    weights: Vec<f64>,
   132→    /// Number of IRLS iterations
   133→    iterations: usize,
   134→    /// Whether IRLS converged
   135→    converged: bool,
   136→}
   137→
   138→/// Fitted regularized model results (Lasso/Ridge)
   139→#[derive(Debug)]
   140→pub struct RegularizedFit {
   141→    coefficients: Vec<f64>,
   142→    fitted_values: Vec<f64>,
   143→    residuals: Vec<f64>,
   144→    /// Number of observations
   145→    n_obs: usize,
   146→    /// Design matrix for bootstrap resampling
   147→    x_matrix: Mat<f64>,
   148→    /// Response vector for bootstrap resampling
   149→    y_vector: Vec<f64>,
   150→    /// Regularization parameter used
   151→    lambda: f64,
   152→    /// Elastic net mixing parameter (1.0 = Lasso, 0.0 = Ridge)
   153→    alpha: f64,
   154→    /// Number of coordinate descent iterations
   155→    #[allow(dead_code)]
   156→    iterations: usize,
   157→    /// Whether algorithm converged
   158→    #[allow(dead_code)]
   159→    converged: bool,
   160→}
   161→
   162→impl RegularizedFit {
   163→    /// Create a RegularizedFit from coordinate descent results
   164→    pub fn from_coordinate_descent(
   165→        result: optimcore::OptimResult,
   166→        x_matrix: ndarray::Array2<f64>,
   167→        y_vector: ndarray::Array1<f64>,
   168→        lambda: f64,
   169→        alpha: f64,
   170→    ) -> Self {
   171→        let n_obs = x_matrix.nrows();
   172→        
   173→        // Calculate fitted values
   174→        let fitted_values: Vec<f64> = (0..n_obs)
   175→            .map(|i| {
   176→                x_matrix.row(i).iter()
   177→                    .zip(&result.params)
   178→                    .map(|(x, beta)| x * beta)
   179→                    .sum()
   180→            })
   181→            .collect();
   182→
   183→        // Calculate residuals
   184→        let residuals: Vec<f64> = y_vector.iter()
   185→            .zip(&fitted_values)
   186→            .map(|(y, fitted)| y - fitted)
   187→            .collect();
   188→
   189→        // Convert ndarray to faer matrix
   190→        let x_mat = Mat::from_fn(n_obs, x_matrix.ncols(), |i, j| x_matrix[[i, j]]);
   191→        
   192→        RegularizedFit {
   193→            coefficients: result.params.to_vec(),
   194→            fitted_values,
   195→            residuals,
   196→            n_obs,
   197→            x_matrix: x_mat,
   198→            y_vector: y_vector.to_vec(),
   199→            lambda,
   200→            alpha,
   201→            iterations: result.iterations,
   202→            converged: result.converged,
   203→        }
   204→    }
   205→}
   206→
   207→impl Lm {
   208→    /// Fit a linear model using ndarray views (recommended modern API)
   209→    ///
   210→    /// This implementation uses faer's column-pivoted QR decomposition to handle
   211→    /// rank-deficient design matrices robustly, as is common in statistical modeling.
   212→    ///
   213→    /// # Arguments
   214→    /// * `x` - Design matrix as ndarray view (n_observations × n_predictors)
   215→    /// * `y` - Response vector as ndarray view (n_observations)
   216→    ///
   217→    /// # Example
   218→    /// ```
   219→    /// use ndarray::Array2;
   220→    /// use stats_models::Lm;
   221→    ///
   222→    /// // Create design matrix with intercept
   223→    /// let x = Array2::from_shape_vec((3, 2), vec![1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 2.0, 1.0, 3.0]).unwrap();
   224→    /// let y = vec![3.0, 5.0, 7.0];
   225→    ///
   226→    /// let fit = Lm::fit_ndarray(x.view(), ndarray::Array1::from(y).view()).unwrap();
   227→    /// ```
   228→    pub fn fit_ndarray(x: ArrayView2<f64>, y: ArrayView1<f64>) -> Result<LmFit, ModelError> {
   229→        let (n_rows, n_cols) = x.dim();
   230→
   231→        if y.len() != n_rows {
   232→            return Err(ModelError::InvalidModel(
   233→                "Response vector length doesn't match number of observations".to_string(),
   234→            ));
   235→        }
   236→
   237→        // Convert to faer matrices
   238→        let x_mat = Mat::from_fn(n_rows, n_cols, |i, j| x[[i, j]]);
   239→        let y_mat = Mat::from_fn(n_rows, 1, |i, _| y[i]);
   240→
   241→        Self::fit_internal(x_mat, y_mat, n_rows, n_cols)
   242→    }
   243→
   244→    /// Fit a linear model using raw slices (legacy API for backward compatibility)
   245→    ///
   246→    /// This implementation uses faer's column-pivoted QR decomposition to handle
   247→    /// rank-deficient design matrices robustly, as is common in statistical modeling.
   248→    ///
   249→    /// **Note**: Consider using `fit_ndarray` for better type safety and ergonomics.
   250→    pub fn fit(
   251→        x_data: &[f64],
   252→        y_data: &[f64],
   253→        n_rows: usize,
   254→        n_cols: usize,
   255→    ) -> Result<LmFit, ModelError> {
   256→        if x_data.len() != n_rows * n_cols {
   257→            return Err(ModelError::InvalidModel(
   258→                "Design matrix dimensions don't match data".to_string(),
   259→            ));
   260→        }
   261→
   262→        if y_data.len() != n_rows {
   263→            return Err(ModelError::InvalidModel(
   264→                "Response vector length doesn't match number of observations".to_string(),
   265→            ));
   266→        }
   267→
   268→        // Create faer matrices from input data
   269→        let x_mat = Mat::from_fn(n_rows, n_cols, |i, j| x_data[i * n_cols + j]);
   270→        let y_mat = Mat::from_fn(n_rows, 1, |i, _| y_data[i]);
   271→
   272→        Self::fit_internal(x_mat, y_mat, n_rows, n_cols)
   273→    }
   274→
   275→    /// Internal fitting logic shared by both APIs
   276→    fn fit_internal(
   277→        x_mat: Mat<f64>,
   278→        y_mat: Mat<f64>,
   279→        n_rows: usize,
   280→        n_cols: usize,
   281→    ) -> Result<LmFit, ModelError> {
   282→        // Use column-pivoted QR for numerical stability with rank-deficient matrices
   283→        let qr = x_mat.col_piv_qr();
   284→
   285→        // Calculate rank from R matrix diagonal elements
   286→        let r_matrix = qr.R();
   287→        let tolerance = 1e-12;
   288→        let mut rank = 0;
   289→        for i in 0..r_matrix.nrows().min(r_matrix.ncols()) {
   290→            if r_matrix[(i, i)].abs() > tolerance {
   291→                rank += 1;
   292→            }
   293→        }
   294→
   295→        // Handle rank-deficient matrices by checking for numerical instability
   296→        let coefficients_mat = qr.solve_lstsq(&y_mat);
   297→
   298→        // Check for numerical instability and apply correction if needed
   299→        let has_instability =
   300→            (0..coefficients_mat.nrows()).any(|i| coefficients_mat[(i, 0)].abs() > 1e10);
   301→
   302→        let final_coefficients_mat = if has_instability {
   303→            // For rank-deficient case, set coefficients for columns beyond rank to zero
   304→            let mut corrected_coefs = Vec::new();
   305→            for i in 0..coefficients_mat.nrows() {
   306→                let coef = coefficients_mat[(i, 0)];
   307→                if coef.abs() > 1e10 {
   308→                    corrected_coefs.push(0.0);
   309→                } else {
   310→                    corrected_coefs.push(coef);
   311→                }
   312→            }
   313→            Mat::from_fn(n_cols, 1, |i, _| corrected_coefs[i])
   314→        } else {
   315→            coefficients_mat
   316→        };
   317→
   318→        // Calculate fitted values: ŷ = X * β (using corrected coefficients)
   319→        let fitted_mat = &x_mat * &final_coefficients_mat;
   320→
   321→        // Calculate residuals: e = y - ŷ
   322→        let residuals_mat = &y_mat - &fitted_mat;
   323→
   324→        // Extract results to plain Vec<f64> for API
   325→        let coefficients: Vec<f64> = (0..final_coefficients_mat.nrows())
   326→            .map(|i| final_coefficients_mat[(i, 0)])
   327→            .collect();
   328→
   329→        let fitted_values: Vec<f64> = (0..fitted_mat.nrows())
   330→            .map(|i| fitted_mat[(i, 0)])
   331→            .collect();
   332→
   333→        let residuals: Vec<f64> = (0..residuals_mat.nrows())
   334→            .map(|i| residuals_mat[(i, 0)])
   335→            .collect();
   336→
   337→        // rank is already computed above using qr.rank()
   338→
   339→        Ok(LmFit {
   340→            coefficients,
   341→            fitted_values,
   342→            residuals,
   343→            n_obs: n_rows,
   344→            rank,
   345→            x_matrix: x_mat,
   346→        })
   347→    }
   348→}
   349→
   350→impl LmFit {
   351→    /// Get the number of observations used in the model
   352→    pub fn n_obs(&self) -> usize {
   353→        self.n_obs
   354→    }
   355→
   356→    /// Get the rank of the design matrix (number of linearly independent columns)
   357→    pub fn rank(&self) -> usize {
   358→        self.rank
   359→    }
   360→
   361→    /// Get degrees of freedom for error (n_obs - rank)
   362→    pub fn df_resid(&self) -> usize {
   363→        self.n_obs.saturating_sub(self.rank)
   364→    }
   365→}

   367→impl Glm {
   368→    /// Fit a generalized linear model using ndarray views
   369→    ///
   370→    /// This implementation uses the IRLS algorithm to fit GLMs with any
   371→    /// exponential family distribution and link function.
   372→    ///
   373→    /// # Arguments
   374→    /// * `x` - Design matrix as ndarray view (n_observations × n_predictors)
   375→    /// * `y` - Response vector as ndarray view (n_observations)
   376→    /// * `family` - Exponential family (Gaussian, Poisson, Binomial)
   377→    /// * `start` - Optional starting values for coefficients
   378→    /// * `config` - Optional IRLS configuration
   379→    pub fn fit_ndarray<F>(
   380→        x: ArrayView2<f64>,
   381→        y: ArrayView1<f64>,
   382→        family: F,
   383→        start: Option<Array1<f64>>,
   384→        config: Option<IrlsConfig>,
   385→    ) -> Result<GlmFit, ModelError>
   386→    where
   387→        F: Family,
   388→    {
   389→        let (n_rows, n_cols) = x.dim();
   390→
   391→        if y.len() != n_rows {
   392→            return Err(ModelError::InvalidModel(
   393→                "Response vector length doesn't match number of observations".to_string(),
   394→            ));
   395→        }
   396→
   397→        let link = family.link();
   398→
   399→        // Define closures for IRLS algorithm
   400→        let link_fn = |mu: f64| link.link(mu);
   401→        let inverse_link_fn = |eta: f64| link.inverse_link(eta);
   402→        let mu_eta_fn = |eta: f64| link.mu_eta(eta);
   403→        let variance_fn = |mu: f64| family.variance(mu);
   404→        let dev_resid_fn = |y: f64, mu: f64| family.dev_resid(y, mu);
   405→
   406→        let functions = optimcore::GlmFunctions {
   407→            link_fn,
   408→            inverse_link_fn,
   409→            mu_eta_fn,
   410→            variance_fn,
   411→            dev_resid_fn,
   412→        };
   413→
   414→        // Run IRLS algorithm
   415→        let irls_result = irls(x, y, functions, start, config)
   416→            .map_err(|e| ModelError::LinearAlgebra(e.to_string()))?;
   417→
   418→        // Calculate fitted values: μ = g⁻¹(X * β)
   419→        let fitted_values: Vec<f64> = irls_result
   420→            .linear_predictor
   421→            .iter()
   422→            .map(|&eta| inverse_link_fn(eta))
   423→            .collect();
   424→
   425→        // Calculate residuals: e = y - μ
   426→        let residuals: Vec<f64> = y
   427→            .iter()
   428→            .zip(fitted_values.iter())
   429→            .map(|(&y_val, &fitted)| y_val - fitted)
   430→            .collect();
   431→
   432→        // Convert ndarray to faer matrix for storage
   433→        let x_matrix = Mat::from_fn(n_rows, n_cols, |i, j| x[[i, j]]);
   434→
   435→        Ok(GlmFit {
   436→            coefficients: irls_result.params.to_vec(),
   437→            fitted_values,
   438→            residuals,
   439→            linear_predictor: irls_result.linear_predictor.to_vec(),
   440→            n_obs: n_rows,
   441→            x_matrix,
   442→            deviance: irls_result.deviance,
   443→            weights: irls_result.weights.to_vec(),
   444→            iterations: irls_result.iterations,
   445→            converged: irls_result.converged,
   446→        })
   447→    }
   448→}

   482→impl Lasso {
   483→    /// Fit a Lasso regression model using coordinate descent
   484→    ///
   485→    /// This implementation uses coordinate descent optimization with L1 regularization
   486→    /// to encourage sparse solutions.
   487→    ///
   488→    /// # Arguments
   489→    /// * `x` - Design matrix as ndarray view (n_observations × n_predictors)
   490→    /// * `y` - Response vector as ndarray view (n_observations)
   491→    /// * `lambda` - L1 regularization parameter (higher values = more regularization)
   492→    /// * `config` - Optional coordinate descent configuration
   493→    pub fn fit(
   494→        x: ArrayView2<f64>,
   495→        y: ArrayView1<f64>,
   496→        lambda: f64,
   497→        config: Option<CoordinateDescentConfig>,
   498→    ) -> Result<RegularizedFit, ModelError> {
   499→        let mut config = config.unwrap_or_default();
   500→        config.lambda = lambda;
   501→        config.alpha = 1.0; // Pure Lasso (L1 only)
   502→
   503→        let result = coordinate_descent(x, y, Some(config))
   504→            .map_err(|e| ModelError::LinearAlgebra(e.to_string()))?;
   505→
   506→        let fitted_values: Vec<f64> = result.linear_predictor.to_vec();
   507→        let residuals: Vec<f64> = y
   508→            .iter()
   509→            .zip(fitted_values.iter())
   510→            .map(|(&y_val, &fitted)| y_val - fitted)
   511→            .collect();
   512→
   513→        // Store matrices for bootstrap resampling
   514→        let x_matrix = Mat::from_fn(x.nrows(), x.ncols(), |i, j| x[[i, j]]);
   515→        let y_vector = y.to_vec();
   516→
   517→        Ok(RegularizedFit {
   518→            coefficients: result.params.to_vec(),
   519→            fitted_values,
   520→            residuals,
   521→            n_obs: x.nrows(),
   522→            x_matrix,
   523→            y_vector,
   524→            lambda,
   525→            alpha: 1.0,
   526→            iterations: result.iterations,
   527→            converged: result.converged,
   528→        })
   529→    }
   530→}

   533→    /// Fit a Ridge regression model using coordinate descent

   536→    /// to shrink coefficients toward zero without enforcing sparsity.
   537→    ///

   540→    /// * `y` - Response vector as ndarray view (n_observations)

   542→    /// * `config` - Optional coordinate descent configuration

   548→    ) -> Result<RegularizedFit, ModelError> {
   549→        let mut config = config.unwrap_or_default();
   550→        config.lambda = lambda;
   551→        config.alpha = 0.0; // Pure Ridge (L2 only)
   552→
   553→        let result = coordinate_descent(x, y, Some(config))
   554→            .map_err(|e| ModelError::LinearAlgebra(e.to_string()))?;
   555→
   556→        let fitted_values: Vec<f64> = result.linear_predictor.to_vec();
   557→        let residuals: Vec<f64> = y
   558→            .iter()
   559→            .zip(fitted_values.iter())
   560→            .map(|(&y_val, &fitted)| y_val - fitted)
   561→            .collect();
   562→
   563→        // Store matrices for bootstrap resampling
   564→        let x_matrix = Mat::from_fn(x.nrows(), x.ncols(), |i, j| x[[i, j]]);
   565→        let y_vector = y.to_vec();
   566→
   567→        Ok(RegularizedFit {
   568→            coefficients: result.params.to_vec(),
   569→            fitted_values,
   570→            residuals,
   571→            n_obs: x.nrows(),
   572→            x_matrix,
   573→            y_vector,

   575→            alpha: 0.0,
   576→            iterations: result.iterations,
   577→            converged: result.converged,
   578→        })
   579→    }

   582→impl Fit for LmFit {
   583→    fn coefficients(&self) -> &[f64] {
   584→        &self.coefficients
   585→    }
   586→
   587→    fn fitted(&self) -> &[f64] {
   588→        &self.fitted_values
   589→    }
   590→
   591→    fn residuals(&self) -> &[f64] {
   592→        &self.residuals
   593→    }
   594→
   595→    fn summary(&self) -> ModelSummary {
   596→        use inference::{f_test_p_value, model_f_statistic, t_statistics, t_test_p_values};
   597→
   598→        // Calculate residual standard error: s² = RSS / (n - p)
   599→        let rss: f64 = self.residuals.iter().map(|r| r * r).sum();
   600→        let df_resid = self.df_resid() as f64;
   601→        let mse = if df_resid > 0.0 { rss / df_resid } else { 0.0 };
   602→        let residual_std_error = mse.sqrt();
   603→
   604→        // Calculate Total Sum of Squares (TSS) and R-squared
   605→        let y_mean: f64 = self
   606→            .fitted_values
   607→            .iter()
   608→            .zip(self.residuals.iter())
   609→            .map(|(fitted, resid)| fitted + resid) // y = fitted + residual
   610→            .sum::<f64>()
   611→            / self.n_obs as f64;
   612→
   613→        let tss: f64 = self
   614→            .fitted_values
   615→            .iter()
   616→            .zip(self.residuals.iter())
   617→            .map(|(fitted, resid)| {
   618→                let y = fitted + resid;
   619→                (y - y_mean).powi(2)
   620→            })
   621→            .sum();
   622→
   623→        let r_squared = if tss > 0.0 { 1.0 - rss / tss } else { 0.0 };
   624→
   625→        // Adjusted R-squared: 1 - (RSS/(n-p)) / (TSS/(n-1))
   626→        let n = self.n_obs as f64;
   627→        let p = self.rank as f64;
   628→        let adj_r_squared = if n > 1.0 && p > 0.0 {
   629→            1.0 - (rss / (n - p)) / (tss / (n - 1.0))
   630→        } else {
   631→            r_squared
   632→        };
   633→
   634→        // F-statistic for overall model significance
   635→        // RSS for null model (intercept only) equals TSS
   636→        let df_model = if self.rank > 1 { self.rank - 1 } else { 0 }; // exclude intercept
   637→        let f_statistic = if df_model > 0 {
   638→            model_f_statistic(rss, tss, df_model, self.df_resid())
   639→        } else {
   640→            0.0
   641→        };
   642→
   643→        let f_p_value = if df_model > 0 && self.df_resid() > 0 {
   644→            f_test_p_value(f_statistic, df_model as i32, self.df_resid() as i32)
   645→        } else {
   646→            1.0
   647→        };
   648→
   649→        // Calculate variance-covariance matrix: σ² * (X'X)^(-1)
   650→        // Compute X'X
   651→        let xtx = self.x_matrix.transpose() * &self.x_matrix;
   652→
   653→        // Compute (X'X)^(-1) - use QR decomposition for numerical stability
   654→        let qr = xtx.col_piv_qr();
   655→        let identity = Mat::identity(xtx.nrows(), xtx.ncols());
   656→        let xtx_inv = qr.solve(&identity);
   657→
   658→        // Standard errors are sqrt of diagonal elements of σ² * (X'X)^(-1)
   659→        let std_errors: Vec<f64> = (0..self.coefficients.len())
   660→            .map(|i| (mse * xtx_inv[(i, i)]).sqrt())
   661→            .collect();
   662→
   663→        let t_stats = t_statistics(&self.coefficients, &std_errors);
   664→        let p_values = t_test_p_values(&t_stats, self.df_resid() as i32);
   665→
   666→        let mut coefficient_rows = Vec::new();
   667→        for (i, &coef) in self.coefficients.iter().enumerate() {
   668→            let name = if i == 0 {
   669→                "Intercept".to_string()
   670→            } else {
   671→                format!("X{}", i)
   672→            };
   673→
   674→            coefficient_rows.push(CoefficientRow {
   675→                name,
   676→                estimate: coef,
   677→                std_error: std_errors[i],
   678→                t_value: t_stats[i],
   679→                p_value: p_values[i],
   680→            });
   681→        }
   682→
   683→        ModelSummary {
   684→            coefficients: coefficient_rows,
   685→            r_squared,
   686→            adj_r_squared,
   687→            residual_std_error,
   688→            f_statistic,
   689→            f_p_value,
   690→            df_model,
   691→            df_residual: self.df_resid(),
   692→        }
   693→    }
   694→
   695→    fn bootstrap(&self, n_samples: usize) -> Result<BootstrapResult, ModelError> {
   696→        // Create bootstrap statistic that refits the linear model
   697→        let bootstrap_stat = LmBootstrapStatistic {
   698→            n_params: self.coefficients.len(),
   699→        };
   700→
   701→        let config = BootstrapConfig {
   702→            n_samples,
   703→            seed: Some(42), // For reproducible results
   704→            parallel: true,
   705→        };
   706→
   707→        // Convert faer matrix to ndarray
   708→        let n_cols = self.x_matrix.ncols();
   709→        let mut x_data = Vec::with_capacity(self.n_obs * n_cols);
   710→        for i in 0..self.n_obs {
   711→            for j in 0..n_cols {
   712→                x_data.push(self.x_matrix[(i, j)]);
   713→            }
   714→        }
   715→        let x_view =
   716→            ndarray::ArrayView2::from_shape((self.n_obs, n_cols), &x_data)
   717→                .map_err(|e| ModelError::LinearAlgebra(e.to_string()))?;
   718→
   719→        // Reconstruct y from fitted values and residuals
   720→        let y_data: Vec<f64> = self.fitted_values.iter()
   721→            .zip(self.residuals.iter())
   722→            .map(|(fitted, resid)| fitted + resid)
   723→            .collect();
   724→        let y_view = ndarray::ArrayView1::from(&y_data);
   725→
   726→        bootstrap(x_view, y_view, bootstrap_stat, Some(config))
   727→            .map_err(|e| ModelError::LinearAlgebra(e.to_string()))
   728→    }
   729→}
   730→
   731→impl Fit for GlmFit {
   732→    fn coefficients(&self) -> &[f64] {
   733→        &self.coefficients
   734→    }
   735→
   736→    fn fitted(&self) -> &[f64] {
   737→        &self.fitted_values
   738→    }
   739→
   740→    fn residuals(&self) -> &[f64] {
   741→        &self.residuals
   742→    }
   743→
   744→    fn summary(&self) -> ModelSummary {
   745→        use inference::{f_test_p_value, model_f_statistic, t_statistics, t_test_p_values};
   746→
   747→        // For GLM, use deviance-based statistics
   748→        let rss: f64 = self.residuals.iter().map(|r| r * r).sum();
   749→        let n = self.n_obs as f64;
   750→        let p = self.coefficients.len() as f64;
   751→        let df_resid = (n - p).max(0.0);
   752→
   753→        // Use deviance for dispersion estimation in GLM
   754→        let dispersion = if df_resid > 0.0 {
   755→            self.deviance / df_resid
   756→        } else {
   757→            1.0
   758→        };
   759→        let residual_std_error = dispersion.sqrt();
   760→
   761→        // For GLM, R² is calculated differently (based on deviance)
   762→        // Null deviance would be calculated with intercept-only model
   763→        // For now, use a simplified version
   764→        let y_mean: f64 = self
   765→            .fitted_values
   766→            .iter()
   767→            .zip(self.residuals.iter())
   768→            .map(|(fitted, resid)| fitted + resid)
   769→            .sum::<f64>()
   770→            / n;
   771→
   772→        let tss: f64 = self
   773→            .fitted_values
   774→            .iter()
   775→            .zip(self.residuals.iter())
   776→            .map(|(fitted, resid)| {
   777→                let y = fitted + resid;
   778→                (y - y_mean).powi(2)
   779→            })
   780→            .sum();
   781→
   782→        let r_squared = if tss > 0.0 { 1.0 - rss / tss } else { 0.0 };
   783→        let adj_r_squared = if n > 1.0 && p > 0.0 {
   784→            1.0 - (rss / df_resid) / (tss / (n - 1.0))
   785→        } else {
   786→            r_squared
   787→        };
   788→
   789→        // F-statistic for overall model significance
   790→        let df_model = if p > 1.0 { p as usize - 1 } else { 0 }; // exclude intercept
   791→        let f_statistic = if df_model > 0 {
   792→            model_f_statistic(rss, tss, df_model, df_resid as usize)
   793→        } else {
   794→            0.0
   795→        };
   796→
   797→        let f_p_value = if df_model > 0 && df_resid > 0.0 {
   798→            f_test_p_value(f_statistic, df_model as i32, df_resid as i32)
   799→        } else {
   800→            1.0
   801→        };
   802→
   803→        // Calculate variance-covariance matrix using weights
   804→        // For GLM: Var(β̂) = dispersion * (X'WX)^(-1)
   805→        let n_vars = self.coefficients.len();
   806→        let x_weighted = Mat::from_fn(self.n_obs, n_vars, |i, j| {
   807→            self.weights[i].sqrt() * self.x_matrix[(i, j)]
   808→        });
   809→
   810→        let xtx = x_weighted.transpose() * &x_weighted;
   811→        let qr = xtx.col_piv_qr();
   812→        let identity = Mat::identity(xtx.nrows(), xtx.ncols());
   813→        let xtx_inv = qr.solve(&identity);
   814→
   815→        // Standard errors for GLM
   816→        let std_errors: Vec<f64> = (0..self.coefficients.len())
   817→            .map(|i| (dispersion * xtx_inv[(i, i)]).sqrt())
   818→            .collect();
   819→
   820→        let t_stats = t_statistics(&self.coefficients, &std_errors);
   821→        let p_values = t_test_p_values(&t_stats, df_resid as i32);
   822→
   823→        let mut coefficient_rows = Vec::new();
   824→        for (i, &coef) in self.coefficients.iter().enumerate() {
   825→            let name = if i == 0 {
   826→                "Intercept".to_string()
   827→            } else {
   828→                format!("X{}", i)
   829→            };
   830→
   831→            coefficient_rows.push(CoefficientRow {
   832→                name,
   833→                estimate: coef,
   834→                std_error: std_errors[i],
   835→                t_value: t_stats[i],
   836→                p_value: p_values[i],
   837→            });
   838→        }
   839→
   840→        ModelSummary {
   841→            coefficients: coefficient_rows,
   842→            r_squared,
   843→            adj_r_squared,
   844→            residual_std_error,
   845→            f_statistic,
   846→            f_p_value,
   847→            df_model,
   848→            df_residual: df_resid as usize,
   849→        }
   850→    }
   851→
   852→    fn bootstrap(&self, n_samples: usize) -> Result<BootstrapResult, ModelError> {
   853→        // Create bootstrap statistic that refits the GLM
   854→        let bootstrap_stat = GlmBootstrapStatistic {
   855→            n_params: self.coefficients.len(),
   856→            family: "gaussian".to_string(), // TODO: Store actual family type
   857→        };
   858→
   859→        let config = BootstrapConfig {
   860→            n_samples,
   861→            seed: Some(42), // For reproducible results
   862→            parallel: true,
   863→        };
   864→
   865→        // Convert faer matrix to ndarray
   866→        let n_cols = self.x_matrix.ncols();
   867→        let mut x_data = Vec::with_capacity(self.n_obs * n_cols);
   868→        for i in 0..self.n_obs {
   869→            for j in 0..n_cols {
   870→                x_data.push(self.x_matrix[(i, j)]);
   871→            }
   872→        }
   873→        let x_view =
   874→            ndarray::ArrayView2::from_shape((self.n_obs, n_cols), &x_data)
   875→                .map_err(|e| ModelError::LinearAlgebra(e.to_string()))?;
   876→
   877→        // Reconstruct y from fitted values and residuals
   878→        let y_data: Vec<f64> = self.fitted_values.iter()
   879→            .zip(self.residuals.iter())
   880→            .map(|(fitted, resid)| fitted + resid)
   881→            .collect();
   882→        let y_view = ndarray::ArrayView1::from(&y_data);
   883→
   884→        bootstrap(x_view, y_view, bootstrap_stat, Some(config))
   885→            .map_err(|e| ModelError::LinearAlgebra(e.to_string()))
   886→    }
   887→}

   889→impl Fit for RegularizedFit {
   890→    fn coefficients(&self) -> &[f64] {
   891→        &self.coefficients
   892→    }
   893→
   894→    fn fitted(&self) -> &[f64] {
   895→        &self.fitted_values
   896→    }
   897→
   898→    fn residuals(&self) -> &[f64] {
   899→        &self.residuals
   900→    }
   901→
   902→    fn summary(&self) -> ModelSummary {
   903→        // For regularized models, traditional statistical inference is not applicable
   904→        // since the coefficients are biased by design. We provide basic fit statistics.
   905→        let rss: f64 = self.residuals.iter().map(|r| r * r).sum();
   906→        let n = self.n_obs as f64;
   907→        let p = self.coefficients.len() as f64;
   908→        let df_resid = (n - p).max(0.0);
   909→
   910→        let residual_std_error = if df_resid > 0.0 {
   911→            (rss / df_resid).sqrt()
   912→        } else {
   913→            0.0
   914→        };
   915→
   916→        // Calculate R² for goodness of fit
   917→        let y_mean: f64 = self
   918→            .fitted_values
   919→            .iter()
   920→            .zip(self.residuals.iter())
   921→            .map(|(fitted, resid)| fitted + resid)
   922→            .sum::<f64>()
   923→            / n;
   924→
   925→        let tss: f64 = self
   926→            .fitted_values
   927→            .iter()
   928→            .zip(self.residuals.iter())
   929→            .map(|(fitted, resid)| {
   930→                let y = fitted + resid;
   931→                (y - y_mean).powi(2)
   932→            })
   933→            .sum();
   934→
   935→        let r_squared = if tss > 0.0 { 1.0 - rss / tss } else { 0.0 };
   936→        let adj_r_squared = if n > 1.0 && p > 0.0 {
   937→            1.0 - (rss / df_resid) / (tss / (n - 1.0))
   938→        } else {
   939→            r_squared
   940→        };
   941→
   942→        // For regularized models, we don't provide p-values as they're not meaningful
   943→        let mut coefficient_rows = Vec::new();
   944→        for (i, &coef) in self.coefficients.iter().enumerate() {
   945→            let name = if i == 0 {
   946→                "Intercept".to_string()
   947→            } else {
   948→                format!("X{}", i)
   949→            };
   950→
   951→            coefficient_rows.push(CoefficientRow {
   952→                name,
   953→                estimate: coef,
   954→                std_error: f64::NAN, // Not meaningful for regularized models
   955→                t_value: f64::NAN,   // Not meaningful for regularized models
   956→                p_value: f64::NAN,   // Not meaningful for regularized models
   957→            });
   958→        }
   959→
   960→        ModelSummary {
   961→            coefficients: coefficient_rows,
   962→            r_squared,
   963→            adj_r_squared,
   964→            residual_std_error,
   965→            f_statistic: f64::NAN, // Not meaningful for regularized models
   966→            f_p_value: f64::NAN,   // Not meaningful for regularized models
   967→            df_model: 0,           // Not meaningful for regularized models
   968→            df_residual: df_resid as usize,
   969→        }
   970→    }
   971→
   972→    fn bootstrap(&self, n_samples: usize) -> Result<BootstrapResult, ModelError> {
   973→        // Create bootstrap statistic that refits the regularized model
   974→        let bootstrap_stat = RegularizedBootstrapStatistic {
   975→            lambda: self.lambda,
   976→            alpha: self.alpha,
   977→            n_params: self.coefficients.len(),
   978→        };
   979→
   980→        let config = BootstrapConfig {
   981→            n_samples,
   982→            seed: Some(42), // For reproducible results
   983→            parallel: true,
   984→        };
   985→
   986→        // Convert faer matrix to ndarray
   987→        let n_cols = self.x_matrix.ncols();
   988→        let mut x_data = Vec::with_capacity(self.n_obs * n_cols);
   989→        for i in 0..self.n_obs {
   990→            for j in 0..n_cols {
   991→                x_data.push(self.x_matrix[(i, j)]);
   992→            }
   993→        }
   994→        let x_view =
   995→            ndarray::ArrayView2::from_shape((self.n_obs, n_cols), &x_data)
   996→                .map_err(|e| ModelError::LinearAlgebra(e.to_string()))?;
   997→
   998→        let y_view = ndarray::ArrayView1::from(&self.y_vector);
   999→
  1000→        bootstrap(x_view, y_view, bootstrap_stat, Some(config))
  1001→            .map_err(|e| ModelError::LinearAlgebra(e.to_string()))
  1002→    }
  1003→}
  1004→
  1005→/// Bootstrap statistic for linear models
  1006→struct LmBootstrapStatistic {
  1007→    n_params: usize,
  1008→}
  1009→
  1010→impl BootstrapStatistic for LmBootstrapStatistic {
  1011→    fn compute(&self, x: ArrayView2<f64>, y: ArrayView1<f64>) -> Array1<f64> {
  1012→        match Lm::fit_ndarray(x, y) {
  1013→            Ok(fit) => Array1::from(fit.coefficients().to_vec()),
  1014→            Err(_) => Array1::zeros(self.n_params), // Return zeros with consistent dimension
  1015→        }
  1016→    }
  1017→
  1018→    fn n_stats(&self) -> usize {
  1019→        self.n_params
  1020→    }
  1021→}
  1022→
  1023→/// Bootstrap statistic for generalized linear models
  1024→struct GlmBootstrapStatistic {
  1025→    n_params: usize,
  1026→    #[allow(dead_code)]
  1027→    family: String, // TODO: Replace with actual family type
  1028→}
  1029→
  1030→impl BootstrapStatistic for GlmBootstrapStatistic {
  1031→    fn compute(&self, x: ArrayView2<f64>, y: ArrayView1<f64>) -> Array1<f64> {
  1032→        // For now, use Gaussian family (identity link)
  1033→        match Glm::fit_ndarray(x, y, Gaussian, None, None) {
  1034→            Ok(fit) => Array1::from(fit.coefficients().to_vec()),
  1035→            Err(_) => Array1::zeros(self.n_params), // Return zeros with consistent dimension
  1036→        }
  1037→    }
  1038→
  1039→    fn n_stats(&self) -> usize {
  1040→        self.n_params
  1041→    }
  1042→}
  1043→
  1044→/// Bootstrap statistic for regularized models
  1045→struct RegularizedBootstrapStatistic {
  1046→    lambda: f64,
  1047→    alpha: f64,
  1048→    n_params: usize,
  1049→}
  1050→
  1051→impl BootstrapStatistic for RegularizedBootstrapStatistic {
  1052→    fn compute(&self, x: ArrayView2<f64>, y: ArrayView1<f64>) -> Array1<f64> {
  1053→        let config = CoordinateDescentConfig {
  1054→            lambda: self.lambda,
  1055→            alpha: self.alpha,
  1056→            ..Default::default()
  1057→        };
  1058→
  1059→        match coordinate_descent(x, y, Some(config)) {
  1060→            Ok(result) => result.params,
  1061→            Err(_) => Array1::zeros(self.n_params), // Return zeros with consistent dimension
  1062→        }
  1063→    }
  1064→
  1065→    fn n_stats(&self) -> usize {
  1066→        self.n_params
  1067→    }
  1068→}
  1069→
  1070→/// Convenience macro for fitting generalized linear models with optional regularization
  1071→///
  1072→/// This macro provides an ergonomic API for fitting GLMs with elastic net regularization
  1073→/// (Lasso and Ridge).
  1074→///
  1075→/// # Examples
  1076→///
  1077→/// ```ignore
  1078→/// // Standard GLM (no regularization)
  1079→/// let fit = glm!(y ~ x1 + x2, data = df, family = Poisson);
  1080→///
  1081→/// // Lasso regression (alpha = 1.0)
  1082→/// let fit = glm!(y ~ x1 + x2, data = df, family = Gaussian, alpha = 1.0, lambda = 0.1);
  1083→///
  1084→/// // Ridge regression (alpha = 0.0)
  1085→/// let fit = glm!(y ~ x1 + x2, data = df, family = Gaussian, alpha = 0.0, lambda = 0.5);
  1086→///
  1087→/// // Elastic net (0 < alpha < 1)
  1088→/// let fit = glm!(y ~ x1 + x2, data = df, family = Gaussian, alpha = 0.5, lambda = 0.2);
  1089→/// ```
  1090→#[macro_export]
  1091→macro_rules! glm {
  1092→    // GLM with regularization
  1093→    ($formula:expr, data = $data:expr, family = $family:expr, alpha = $alpha:expr, lambda = $lambda:expr) => {{
  1094→        // Parse formula and build design matrix
  1095→        let model_spec = $formula; // Assume formula is already parsed
  1096→        let x_matrix = $data.design_matrix(&model_spec)?;
  1097→        let y_vector = $data.response(&model_spec)?;
  1098→        
  1099→        // Decide which model to use based on alpha and lambda
  1100→        if $lambda > 0.0 {
  1101→            if $alpha == 1.0 {
  1102→                // Pure Lasso
  1103→                $crate::Lasso::fit(x_matrix.view(), y_vector.view(), $lambda, None)
  1104→            } else if $alpha == 0.0 {
  1105→                // Pure Ridge
  1106→                $crate::Ridge::fit(x_matrix.view(), y_vector.view(), $lambda, None)
  1107→            } else {
  1108→                // Elastic net - use coordinate descent with custom alpha
  1109→                let config = ::optimcore::CoordinateDescentConfig {
  1110→                    lambda: $lambda,
  1111→                    alpha: $alpha,
  1112→                    ..Default::default()
  1113→                };
  1114→                let result = ::optimcore::coordinate_descent(
  1115→                    x_matrix.view(), 
  1116→                    y_vector.view(), 
  1117→                    Some(config)
  1118→                )?;
  1119→                Ok($crate::RegularizedFit::from_coordinate_descent(
  1120→                    result,
  1121→                    x_matrix,
  1122→                    y_vector,
  1123→                    $lambda,
  1124→                    $alpha
  1125→                ))
  1126→            }
  1127→        } else {
  1128→            // No regularization - use standard GLM
  1129→            $crate::Glm::fit_ndarray(
  1130→                x_matrix.view(),
  1131→                y_vector.view(),
  1132→                $family,
  1133→                None,
  1134→                None
  1135→            )
  1136→        }
  1137→    }};
  1138→    
  1139→    // GLM without regularization
  1140→    ($formula:expr, data = $data:expr, family = $family:expr) => {{
  1141→        glm!($formula, data = $data, family = $family, alpha = 1.0, lambda = 0.0)
  1142→    }};
  1143→}

  1345→    #[test]
  1346→    fn test_multicollinearity_debug() {
  1347→        // Test with non-collinear data to check if our fix works
  1348→        let n_obs = 8;
  1349→        let x1_values: Vec<f64> = (1..=n_obs).map(|i| i as f64).collect();
  1350→        let x2_values: Vec<f64> = (1..=n_obs)
  1351→            .map(|i| (i as f64) * 0.3 + 0.1 * (i as f64).powi(2))
  1352→            .collect();
  1353→        let y_values: Vec<f64> = x1_values
  1354→            .iter()
  1355→            .zip(x2_values.iter())
  1356→            .map(|(&x1, &x2)| 2.0 + 1.5 * x1 + 0.8 * x2 + 0.1 * x1 * x2.sin())
  1357→            .collect();
  1358→
  1359→        // Design matrix: [1, x1, x2] - now without perfect multicollinearity
  1360→        let mut x_data = Vec::with_capacity(n_obs * 3);
  1361→        for i in 0..n_obs {
  1362→            x_data.push(1.0); // intercept
  1363→            x_data.push(x1_values[i]); // x1
  1364→            x_data.push(x2_values[i]); // x2
  1365→        }
  1366→
  1367→        println!("=== Multiple Regression Debug ===");
  1368→        println!("x1: {:?}", x1_values);
  1369→        println!("x2: {:?}", x2_values);
  1370→        println!("x2 is not perfectly correlated with x1");
  1371→
  1372→        let x_mat = Mat::from_fn(n_obs, 3, |i, j| x_data[i * 3 + j]);
  1373→        let _y_mat = Mat::from_fn(n_obs, 1, |i, _| y_values[i]);
  1374→
  1375→        println!("Design matrix X (first 3 rows):");
  1376→        for i in 0..3 {
  1377→            println!(
  1378→                "  [{}, {}, {}]",
  1379→                x_mat[(i, 0)],
  1380→                x_mat[(i, 1)],
  1381→                x_mat[(i, 2)]
  1382→            );
  1383→        }
  1384→
  1385→        let qr = x_mat.col_piv_qr();
  1386→        let r_matrix = qr.R();
  1387→
  1388→        println!("R matrix diagonal:");
  1389→        for i in 0..r_matrix.nrows().min(r_matrix.ncols()) {
  1390→            println!("  R[{},{}] = {}", i, i, r_matrix[(i, i)]);
  1391→        }
  1392→
  1393→        // Detect rank
  1394→        let tolerance = 1e-12;
  1395→        let mut rank = 0;
  1396→        for i in 0..r_matrix.nrows().min(r_matrix.ncols()) {
  1397→            if r_matrix[(i, i)].abs() > tolerance {
  1398→                rank += 1;
  1399→            }
  1400→        }
  1401→        println!("Detected rank: {} (should be 3 for full rank)", rank);
  1402→
  1403→        let fit = Lm::fit(&x_data, &y_values, n_obs, 3).unwrap();
  1404→        println!("Our coefficients: {:?}", fit.coefficients());
  1405→        println!("Our rank: {}", fit.rank());
  1406→
  1407→        // Check if coefficients are numerically stable
  1408→        for (i, &coef) in fit.coefficients().iter().enumerate() {
  1409→            if coef.abs() > 1e6 {
  1410→                println!(
  1411→                    "WARNING: Coefficient {} is numerically unstable: {}",
  1412→                    i, coef
  1413→                );
  1414→            }
  1415→        }
  1416→    }
